channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bought a Clif Bar? You could be paid as part of a class action settlement
item.description: A lawsuit against Clif Bar & Company alleged that Clif Bars and Clif Kid ZBars were unhealthy due to added sugars, despite being advertised with slogans like "Nutrition for Sustained Energy" and "No High Fructose Corn Syrup" on the product's packaging.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KTLA 5

item.title: As search for Helene's victims drags into second week, sheriff says rescuers 'will not rest'
item.description: PENSACOLA, N.C. (AP) — The search for victims of Hurricane Helene dragged into its second week on Friday, as exhausted rescue crews and volunteers continued to work long days — navigating past washed out roads, downed power lines and mudslides — to reach the isolated and the missing.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: For migrant women who land in Colorado looking for jobs, a common answer emerges: No
item.description: As record numbers of South Americans come to the U.S. seeking better economic opportunities, many are landing in communities that are unprepared for them — and sometimes outright hostile.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Assassination attempts and new threats have reshaped how Donald Trump campaigns
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he'd spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Californians' crime concerns put pressure on criminal justice reform and progressive DAs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ten years ago, Alley Bean joined 3.7 million Californians in voting for a measure that downgraded many nonviolent felony crimes to misdemeanors, such as petty shoplifting and drug use, hoping it would lead to a more equitable criminal justice system and help end mass incarceration
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Penn. Republican Senate candidate David McCormick: 'I'm the underdog'
item.description: “I'm the underdog,” David McCormick told NewsNation’s Leland Vittert in a one-on-one interview for “On Balance.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: East Tennessee hospital employee recounts rooftop rescue during flood
item.description: "I think when you start looking at the data and seeing the magnitude of what happened, there's no predictability about that," said Harrah.
item.dc:creator: Alex Libby, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Firefighters union won't endorse in Trump-Harris race
item.description: The International Association of Fire Fighters (IAFF) announced Thursday it would not endorse a candidate in this year's presidential race, becoming the second major union to do so in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Gallego rakes in close to $22M in third quarter for Arizona Senate race
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) raised close to $22 million in the third quarter of fundraising for this year in the Arizona Senate race, his campaign announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend their strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract, a person briefed on the matter says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, WGN 9

item.title: Pennsylvania US Senate: Bob Casey, Dave McCormick trade jabs in debate
item.description: The candidates battled throughout the debate, often claiming the other was lying on the campaign trail and on the debate stage.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Starlink internet service being sent to places hit by Helene
item.description: FEMA said 60 Starlink units have been sent to multiple places devastated by the hurricane, with more to come.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Texas teen abducted from Dallas Mavericks NBA game shares what lured her from dad
item.description: Natalee Cramer, now 18, is speaking out for the first time since she was abducted from a Dallas Mavericks game and allegedly forced into sex trafficking in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue Ridge Parkway to be closed indefinitely, National Park Service says
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal agencies warn of scams in wake of Helene
item.description: “These criminals take advantage of victims before, during, and after a natural disaster strikes, targeting people when they are most vulnerable, and their methods to commit fraud are wide-ranging,” one U.S. Attorney said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is your deadline?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: 10,000 fentanyl pills seized at Nashville bus station
item.description: Officers found a large bundle wrapped in duct tape with 10,000 pills that contained fentanyl, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, NewsNation

item.title: Wawa named No. 1 convenience store in consumer satisfaction survey
item.description: Analysts noted that many of the top-ranked chains specialized in ready-to-eat foods prepared in the stores' kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: Helene waves and storm surge crash into Florida home as owners watch
item.description: Terrifying moments caught on video showed Hurricane Helene’s powerful and deadly storm surge as it crashed into a home in Pinellas County last week.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, Fox 5

item.title: Meet Spot, the LAPD's new crimefighting robot dog
item.description: The futuristic four-legged machine, which boasts the size and agility of a 70-pound golden retriever, has been met with both fascination and concern.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shoppers are panic-buying toilet paper amid port strike, but experts say there’s no need
item.description: “Hope you have some toilet paper stock piled. It’s started. Sam’s and Costco are depleted,” one user said on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: ACLU lawsuit details DWI scheme rocking Albuquerque police
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A civil rights group is suing the city of Albuquerque, its police department and top officials on behalf of a man who was among those arrested on suspicion of driving while intoxicated and allegedly forced to pay bribes to get the charges dropped.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Tropical Storm Leslie forms; Hurricane Kirk strengthens
item.description: Hurricane Kirk is expected to grow larger as it strengthens in the Atlantic, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Helene's death toll passes 150 as crews search for survivors in North Carolina's mountains
item.description: Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris on Tuesday looking in the mountains of western North Carolina for victims of Hurricane Helene, days after the storm carved a deadly and destructive path through the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hair found in truck helps police identify suspect in 1995 disappearance of Morgan Nick, 6
item.description: ALMA, Ark. (AP) — Hair found in a truck once owned by an Arkansas man who died in prison more than two decades ago was the clue investigators needed to connect the dead inmate to the disappearance of a 6-year-old girl who went missing in 1995.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DEMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watchdog blasts DEA for not reporting waterboarding, torture by Latin American partners
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A federal government watchdog is blasting the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration for failing to timely report human rights violations committed by Latin American law enforcement partners who admitted to waterboarding, suffocating and torturing crime suspects.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Publix donates $1 million to support disaster relief following Hurricane Helene
item.description: Florida-based grocery chain, Publix, announced it's making a major contribution to help relief efforts in areas affected by the catastrophic damage left behind by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: More than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it — an unheard of amount of water that has stunned experts.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Helene's death toll rises to more than 130 as supplies are rushed to areas in need
item.description: A crisis unfolded in Asheville, North Carolina, as officials pledged to get more water, food and other supplies to flood-stricken areas without power and cellular service Monday, days after Hurricane Helene ripped across the U.S. Southeast. The death toll from the storm surpassed 130.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
